title: **Balance Sheet Overview – As of March 31, 2026**
content:
The most significant balance-sheet development this period was the growth in investment securities. Total assets stood at $104,989 million as of March 31, 2026, increased 11.7%.

Investment securities grew to $80,815 million, up 25.0%, reflecting a larger base of earning assets. Loans and leases declined to $20,507 million, down 17.5%. Other assets moved more modestly, ending the period at $1,097 million.

Investment securities is now the largest component of the balance sheet. That growth on the asset side was funded primarily through shares/deposits, reflecting how the institution's holdings were paid for during the period.

Total liabilities stood at $48,808 million, decreased 12.3%, led by shares/deposits at $48,291 million, down 12.5%. Accounts payable and other liabilities moved more modestly over the period.

Total equity stood at $15,577 million, increased 2.8%., supported by undivided earnings of $15,577 million and stable reserves. Taken together, investment securities growth was funded mainly through member deposits while loans contracted slightly. Management continues to watch the pace of loan decline relative to deposit reduction.
